# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3.8'

services:
  # Elasticsearch - Log Storage and Search Engine
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=es-node-01
      - cluster.name=elk-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - bootstrap.memory_lock=true
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
      - xpack.license.self_generated.type=basic
      - action.auto_create_index=true
      - indices.query.bool.max_clause_count=8192
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
      nofile:
        soft: 65536
        hard: 65536
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - ./elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:ro
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
      - "9300:9300"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q '\"status\":\"green\"\\|\"status\":\"yellow\"'"
        ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
        reservations:
          memory: 1g

  # Logstash - Log Collection and Parsing Pipeline
  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.12.0
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - "LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
      - xpack.monitoring.enabled=true
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username=elastic
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    volumes:
      - ./logstash/config/logstash.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/logstash.yml:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline/:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/:ro
      - ./logstash/patterns/:/usr/share/logstash/patterns/:ro
      - logstash_data:/usr/share/logstash/data
    ports:
      # Beats input
      - "5044:5044"
      # TCP/UDP syslog input
      - "5514:5514/tcp"
      - "5514:5514/udp"
      # HTTP input for direct log shipping
      - "8080:8080"
      # TCP JSON input
      - "5000:5000/tcp"
      - "5000:5000/udp"
      # Logstash monitoring API
      - "9600:9600"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "curl -s http://localhost:9600/_node/stats | grep -q '\"status\":\"green\"' || curl -s http://localhost:9600 | grep -q 'logstash'"
        ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 90s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
        reservations:
          memory: 512m

  # Kibana - Log Visualization and Dashboard UI
  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.12.0
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME=kibana_system
      - ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY:-a]V>4sp6%kAHf?gY2wML7n+E+rz7/M3}
      - xpack.reporting.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_REPORTING_KEY:-b]V>4sp6%kAHf?gY2wML7n+E+rz7/M3}
      - xpack.security.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_SECURITY_KEY:-c]V>4sp6%kAHf?gY2wML7n+E+rz7/M3}
      - SERVER_NAME=kibana
      - SERVER_HOST=0.0.0.0
      - LOGGING_ROOT_LEVEL=info
      - TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false
    volumes:
      - ./kibana/config/kibana.yml:/usr/share/kibana/config/kibana.yml:ro
      - kibana_data:/usr/share/kibana/data
    ports:
      - "5601:5601"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "curl -s http://localhost:5601/api/status | grep -q 'available' || curl -s http://localhost:5601/api/status | grep -q '\"level\":\"available\"'"
        ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 120s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
        reservations:
          memory: 512m

  # Filebeat - Lightweight Log Shipper (optional agent)
  filebeat:
    image: docker.elastic.co/beats/filebeat:8.12.0
    container_name: filebeat
    user: root
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    volumes:
      - ./filebeat/config/filebeat.yml:/usr/share/filebeat/filebeat.yml:ro
      - filebeat_data:/usr/share/filebeat/data
      # Mount Docker logs for container log collection
      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      # Mount host system logs
      - /var/log:/var/log/host:ro
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
      logstash:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 256m
        reservations:
          memory: 128m

  # Setup service - initializes passwords and roles
  setup:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elk-setup
    volumes:
      - ./setup/entrypoint.sh:/entrypoint.sh:ro
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "/entrypoint.sh"]
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - KIBANA_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - LOGSTASH_PASSWORD=${LOGSTASH_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
    driver: local
    name: elk_elasticsearch_data
  logstash_data:
    driver: local
    name: elk_logstash_data
  kibana_data:
    driver: local
    name: elk_kibana_data
  filebeat_data:
    driver: local
    name: elk_filebeat_data

networks:
  elk-network:
    driver: bridge
    name: elk-network
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: 172.28.0.0/16

# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING CONFIGURATION FILES
# ============================================================
# The following files should be created in the project directory
# ============================================================

# --- File: .env ---
# ELASTIC_PASSWORD=changeme
# KIBANA_PASSWORD=changeme
# LOGSTASH_PASSWORD=changeme
# KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=a_random_32_character_string_here
# KIBANA_REPORTING_KEY=b_random_32_character_string_here
# KIBANA_SECURITY_KEY=c_random_32_character_string_here
# COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=elk

# --- File: elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml ---
# cluster.name: "elk-cluster"
# network.host: 0.0.0.0
# http.port: 9200
# path.data: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
# xpack.security.enabled: true

# --- File: kibana/config/kibana.yml ---
# server.name: kibana
# server.host: "0.0.0.0"
# elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
# elasticsearch.username: "kibana_system"
# elasticsearch.password: "${ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD}"
# xpack.security.enabled: true
# monitoring.ui.container.elasticsearch.enabled: true
# i18n.locale: "en"
# logging:
#   appenders:
#     file:
#       type: file
#       fileName: /var/log/kibana.log
#       layout:
#         type: json
#   root:
#     appenders: [default, file]
#     level: info

# --- File: logstash/config/logstash.yml ---
# http.host: "0.0.0.0"
# path.config: /usr/share/logstash/pipeline
# xpack.monitoring.enabled: true
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username: "elastic"
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password: "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
# log.level: info
# pipeline.workers: 2
# pipeline.batch.size: 125
# pipeline.batch.delay: 50

# --- File: logstash/pipeline/01-input.conf ---
# input {
#   # Beats input (Filebeat, Metricbeat, etc.)
#   beats {
#     port => 5044
#     ssl => false
#   }
#
#   # TCP input for syslog
#   tcp {
#     port => 5514
#     type => "syslog"
#     codec => plain
#   }
#
#   # UDP input for syslog
#   udp {
#     port => 5514
#     type => "syslog"
#     codec => plain
#   }
#
#   # HTTP input for application logs
#   http {
#     port => 8080
#     type => "http-input"
#     codec => json
#   }
#
#   # TCP input for JSON logs
#   tcp {
#     port => 5000
#     type => "json-tcp"
#     codec => json_lines
#   }
# }

# --- File: logstash/pipeline/02-filter.conf ---
# filter {
#   # Parse syslog messages
#   if [type] == "syslog" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => "%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{DATA:syslog_program}(?:\[%{POSINT:syslog_pid}\])?: %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}"
#       }
#       add_field => [ "received_at", "%{@timestamp}" ]
#       add_field => [ "received_from", "%{host}" ]
#     }
#     date {
#       match => [ "syslog_timestamp", "MMM  d HH:mm:ss", "MMM dd HH:mm:ss" ]
#     }
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "log_source" => "syslog" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse Nginx access logs
#   if [fields][log_type] == "nginx-access" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => '%{IPORHOST:remote_addr} - %{DATA:remote_user} \[%{HTTPDATE:time_local}\] "%{WORD:request_method} %{DATA:request_uri} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}" %{NUMBER:status:int} %{NUMBER:body_bytes_sent:int} "%{DATA:http_referer}" "%{DATA:http_user_agent}" "%{DATA:http_x_forwarded_for}"'
#       }
#       remove_field => ["message"]
#     }
#     date {
#       match => ["time_local", "dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss Z"]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#     geoip {
#       source => "remote_addr"
#       target => "geoip"
#     }
#     useragent {
#       source => "http_user_agent"
#       target => "user_agent"
#     }
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "log_source" => "nginx" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse Nginx error logs
#   if [fields][log_type] == "nginx-error" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => "(?<timestamp>%{YEAR}[./]%{MONTHNUM}[./]%{MONTHDAY} %{TIME}) \[%{LOGLEVEL:severity}\] %{POSINT:pid}#%{NUMBER:tid}: \*%{NUMBER:connection_id} %{GREEDYDATA:error_message}"
#       }
#     }
#     date {
#       match => ["timestamp", "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss"]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "log_source" => "nginx-error" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse application JSON logs
#   if [type] == "http-input" or [type] == "json-tcp" {
#     if [message] {
#       json {
#         source => "message"
#         target => "app_data"
#         skip_on_invalid_json => true
#       }
#     }
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "log_source" => "application" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse Apache access logs
#   if [fields][log_type] == "apache-access" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => '%{COMBINEDAPACHELOG}'
#       }
#     }
#     date {
#       match => ["timestamp", "dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss Z"]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#     geoip {
#       source => "clientip"
#       target => "geoip"
#     }
#     mutate {
#       convert => {
#         "response" => "integer"
#         "bytes" => "integer"
#       }
#       add_field => { "log_source" => "apache" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse Docker container logs
#   if [container][name] {
#     mutate {
#       add_field => {
#         "log_source" => "docker"
#         "container_name" => "%{[container][name]}"
#       }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Common enrichment for all logs
#   mutate {
#     add_field => {
#       "environment" => "${ENVIRONMENT:production}"
#       "[@metadata][target_index]" => "logs-%{[log_source]:unknown}-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     }
#     remove_field => ["agent.ephemeral_id", "agent.hostname", "agent.id", "agent.type", "agent.version", "ecs"]
#   }
#
#   # Drop empty or health check logs
#   if [request_uri] =~ /^\/(health|healthz|ready|alive|status)/ {
#     drop {}
#   }
# }

# --- File: logstash/pipeline/03-output.conf ---
# output {
#   # Primary output to Elasticsearch
#   elasticsearch {
#     hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#     user => "elastic"
#     password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#     index => "%{[@metadata][target_index]}"
#     manage_template => true
#     template_overwrite => true
#
#     # ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) settings
#     ilm_enabled => true
#     ilm_rollover_alias => "logs"
#     ilm_pattern => "{now/d}-000001"
#     ilm_policy => "logs-policy"
#   }
#
#   # Debug output (uncomment for troubleshooting)
#   # stdout {
#   #   codec => rubydebug
#   # }
#
#   # Dead letter queue for failed events
#   # Errors are automatically handled by Logstash DLQ
# }

# --- File: logstash/patterns/custom_patterns ---
# NGINX_ACCESS %{IPORHOST:remote_addr} - %{DATA:remote_user} \[%{HTTPDATE:time_local}\] "%{WORD:request_method} %{DATA:request_uri} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}" %{NUMBER:status} %{NUMBER:body_bytes_sent} "%{DATA:http_referer}" "%{DATA:http_user_agent}"
# APP_LOG \[%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:timestamp}\] \[%{LOGLEVEL:level}\] \[%{DATA:service}\] %{GREEDYDATA:log_message}
# CUSTOM_TIMESTAMP %{YEAR}-%{MONTHNUM}-%{MONTHDAY}[T ]%{HOUR}:%{MINUTE}:%{SECOND}(?:\.%{INT})?%{ISO8601_TIMEZONE}?

# --- File: filebeat/config/filebeat.yml ---
# filebeat.inputs:
#   # Collect Docker container logs
#   - type: container
#     paths:
#       - '/var/lib/docker/containers/*/*.log'
#     processors:
#       - add_docker_metadata:
#           host: "unix:///var/run/docker.sock"
#       - decode_json_fields:
#           fields: ["message"]
#           process_array: false
#           max_depth: 3
#           target: ""
#           overwrite_keys: true
#           add_error_key: true
#
#   # Collect host system logs
#   - type: log
#     enabled: true
#     paths:
#       - /var/log/host/syslog
#       - /var/log/host/auth.log
#     fields:
#       log_type: syslog
#     fields_under_root: false
#
# # Ship logs to Logstash
# output.logstash:
#   hosts: ["logstash:5044"]
#   bulk_max_size: 1024
#   slow_start: true
#
# # Logging configuration
# logging.level: info
# logging.to_files: true
# logging.files:
#   path: /usr/share/filebeat/data/logs
#   name: filebeat
#   keepfiles: 7
#   permissions: 0644
#
# # Processors
# processors:
#   - add_host_metadata:
#       when.not.contains.tags: forwarded
#   - add_cloud_metadata: ~
#   - add_docker_metadata: ~

# --- File: setup/entrypoint.sh ---
# #!/bin/bash
# set -e
#
# echo "Waiting for Elasticsearch to be ready..."
# until curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} http://elasticsearch:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q '"status":"green"\|"status":"yellow"'; do
#   echo "Elasticsearch not ready yet. Sleeping..."
#   sleep 5
# done
#
# echo "Setting up kibana_system user password..."
# curl -s -X POST -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/kibana_system/_password \
#   -d "{\"password\": \"${KIBANA_PASSWORD}\"}"
#
# echo ""
# echo "Setting up logstash_system user password..."
# curl -s -X POST -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/logstash_system/_password \
#   -d "{\"password\": \"${LOGSTASH_PASSWORD}\"}"
#
# echo ""
# echo "Creating ILM policy for log rotation..."
# curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_ilm/policy/logs-policy \
#   -d '{
#     "policy": {
#       "phases": {
#         "hot": {
#           "min_age": "0ms",
#           "actions": {
#             "rollover": {
#               "max_age": "1d",
#               "max_primary_shard_size": "10gb"
#             },
#             "set_priority": {
#               "priority": 100
#             }
#           }
#         },
#         "warm": {
#           "min_age": "2d",
#           "actions": {
#             "shrink": {
#               "number_of_shards": 1
#             },
#             "forcemerge": {
#               "max_num_segments": 1
#             },
#             "set_priority": {
#               "priority": 50
#             }
#           }
#         },
#         "cold": {
#           "min_age": "7d",
#           "actions": {
#             "set_priority": {
#               "priority": 0
#             }
#           }
#         },
#         "delete": {
#           "min_age": "30d",
#           "actions": {
#             "delete": {}
#           }
#         }
#       }
#     }
#   }'
#
# echo ""
# echo "Creating index template for logs..."
# curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
#   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#   http://elasticsearch:9200/_index_template/logs-template \
#   -d '{
#     "index_patterns": ["logs-*"],
#     "template": {
#       "settings": {
#         "number_of_shards": 1,
#         "number_of_replicas": 0,
#         "index.lifecycle.name": "logs-policy",
#         "index.lifecycle.rollover_alias": "logs"
#       },
#       "mappings": {
#         "properties": {
#           "@timestamp": { "type": "date" },
#           "message": { "type": "text" },
#           "log_source": { "type": "keyword" },
#           "environment": { "type": "keyword" },
#           "severity": { "type": "keyword" },
#           "container_name": { "type": "keyword" },
#           "status": { "type": "integer" },
#           "geoip": {
#             "properties": {
#               "location": { "type": "geo_point" }
#             }
#           }
#         }
#       }
#     },
#     "priority": 200
#   }'
#
# echo ""
# echo "ELK Stack setup completed successfully!"
